##NAMES=Ferrowyllieite
##RRUFFID=R160068
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=NaNaFe^2+^(Fe^2+^Al)(PO_4_)_3_
##LOCALITY=Victory mine, near Custer, Custer County, South Dakota, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=James Shigley
##DESCRIPTION=Dark green aggregates of blocky crystals, associated with quartz, muscovite, and arrojadite-(BaNa) (R160077)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R160068
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_1.05_Ca_0.07_Mn^2+^_0.29_ □_0.58_)(Fe^2+^_1.87_Mg_0.16_)(Al^3+^_0.72_Fe^3+^_0.28_)(P_1.01_O_4_)_3_ ; Grey phase. Bright phase is arrojadite-(BaNa).
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 11.9493 b: 12.3846 c: 6.3661 alpha: 90 beta: 114.5425 gamma: 90 volume: 856.98 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
